result:
I would make every human mind incapable of sustaining the illusion that anyone else is *less real* than they are. No more adjustable dimmer switch of empathy that lets us turn strangers into statistics, enemies into cartoons, whole populations into background noise. Just the stubborn, unshakeable recognition that every footstep you hear is a universe as intricate as your own, every face you see contains the same dense core of inner weather you carry behind your eyes.

No new laws, no utopian technology—only that one biological patch to the firmware of consciousness. The rest (war, greed, waste, indifference) would start to starve once the basic nutrient of shared reality became indigestible.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
